question: Will Russia enter Khatnie by April 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Khatnie, Kharkiv Oblast, (50.121266° N, 37.565909° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Russia entering Khatnie by the specified date shows low implied probability, driven by stalled Russian advances in eastern Ukraine amid Ukrainian counteroffensives and resource strains from the Kursk incursion. question: Will Russia capture all of Huliaipole by April 30?
description:

If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Once Russia captures the entirety of Huliaipole, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

question: Valorant: MYVRA GC vs No Sweat Blossom (BO3) - VCT Game Changers Latin America North Group Stage
description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between MYVRA GC and No Sweat Blossom in the VCT Game Changers Latin America North Group Stage, initially scheduled for March 16 at 9:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "MYVRA GC" if MYVRA GC win the match against No Sweat Blossom.

This market will resolve to "No Sweat Blossom" if No Sweat Blossom win the match against MYVRA GC.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

question: Will the Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) win the second most seats in the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the second-greatest number of seats in the next Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor) election. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Slovenian Parliament.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Slovenian government, specifically the Slovenian Election Authority (Valmyndigheten) (dvk-rs.si/).

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Slovenian parliamentary election polls from firms like Mediana and Ninamedia consistently place the Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) in a strong second position behind the Freedom Movement (GS), with SDS garnering 20-25% support versus under 15% for rivals like Resni.ca or Democrats, driving its 94% implied probability for second place on Polymarket. This trader consensus reflects SDS's steady base amid Prime Minister Golob's declining approval ratings and government scandals, bolstered by leader Janez Janša's mobilization efforts. Upcoming debates and potential endorsements could reinforce this, though a late GS surge, fragmented opposition consolidation, or unexpected voter turnout shifts might narrow the gap and elevate challengers.

If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Slovenian Parliament.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Slovenian government, specifically the Slovenian Election Authority (Valmyndigheten) (dvk-rs.si/).

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

question: Will the Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) be part of the next Government of Slovenia?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed political party is included in the first Slovenian government formed after the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following a successful confirmation vote in the Slovenian National Assembly (Državni zbor), with ministers sworn in under non-caretaker circumstances.

A party will only be considered part of the coalition if it signs the coalition agreement and provides at least one cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government (e.g., through confidence-and-supply arrangements, parliamentary support agreements, or similar) without signing the coalition agreement and holding a cabinet post will not qualify.

If no government is formed, or the results are not known definitively by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the Government of Slovenia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Slovenian traders on Polymarket are pricing coalition possibilities for the next government following the 2022 parliamentary elections, where Robert Golob's centre-left Freedom Movement (GS) secured 41 seats in a coalition with Social Democrats (SD) and The Left (Levica), holding a slim majority amid ongoing stability challenges. Recent polls from September 2024 show opposition New Slovenia (SDS) leading at 28-32% support, potentially positioning it to lead the next cabinet if snap elections occur or after the 2026 vote, driven by public discontent over economic pressures and corruption probes. Key upcoming events include the October budget vote and any no-confidence motions, which could trigger early polls and shift trader consensus on party inclusions like GS, SDS, or NSi.

question: Will FaZe make a roster move before May?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player joins or leaves FaZe Clan's main CS2 roster (defined as players listed with status = “Starter” in the “Players of FaZe” table on HLTV: https://www.hltv.org/team/6667/faze#tab-rosterBox) by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As of market creation the active starter roster consists of the following five players: broky, karrigan, frozen, jcobbb, and Twistzz. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active starter lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to bench/inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different 5-player active roster on HLTV.

Coach changes and temporary stand-ins for a single event, will not count. For matches and events, the stand-in appears in the active 5-player lineup but with a clear marker (e.g., [SI]) to distinguish them from the core roster.

The primary resolution source for this market will be HLTV; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: FaZe Clan's underwhelming CS2 results, including early exits at PGL Major Copenhagen and IEM Chengdu, fuel trader sentiment for a pre-May roster shakeup, with "Yes" holding a slim 54% implied probability amid tight balance. Recent in-game role experiments by in-game leader karrigan and whispers of rain's benching or buyout talks from insiders like N0tail create uncertainty, but no official announcements from FaZe or HLTV.org confirm action. The crowded transfer window—rival teams like Vitality and G2 also scouting—adds pressure, yet strong showings at upcoming IEM Dallas could solidify the core. A verified signing or loan (e.g., Twistzz rumors) would surge Yes odds; sustained momentum without changes tips toward No.

question: Turkish Constitutional Referendum announced in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a constitutional referendum in Turkey is officially announced by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Constitutional referendum refers to any nationwide vote in Turkey on whether to approve an amendment, or multiple amendments, to the Turkish Constitution.

Officially announced refers to any announcement of a specific date for the referendum, or a definitive announcement that a referendum will be held (e.g. after a constitutional amendment has passed the parliament and been submitted to a referendum by the president).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Turkey; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Turkish Constitutional Referendum announced in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a constitutional referendum in Turkey is officially announced by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Constitutional referendum refers to any nationwide vote in Turkey on whether to approve an amendment, or multiple amendments, to the Turkish Constitution.

Officially announced refers to any announcement of a specific date for the referendum, or a definitive announcement that a referendum will be held (e.g. after a constitutional amendment has passed the parliament and been submitted to a referendum by the president).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Turkey; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' 83% consensus against a Turkish constitutional referendum announcement in 2026 stems primarily from the absence of any official declaration by President Erdogan or the AKP-led coalition, coupled with parliamentary arithmetic hurdles. The ruling alliance holds about 323 seats in the 600-member Grand National Assembly, falling short of the 360 needed to propose amendments for a public vote. Recent local election setbacks in March 2024 eroded AKP momentum, while Erdogan's vague calls for a "civil constitution" lack a 2026 timeline amid economic pressures and Syria operations. No votes or drafts are scheduled, reinforcing skepticism in this mid-term window before 2028 polls.

question: Will turnout in the 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections be below 60%?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve based on the official voter turnout rate for the 2026 Slovenian Parliamentary Election, defined as the total number of ballots cast divided by the total number of eligible voters.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

If the results of this election are not known by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

This market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Slovenian Government, specifically the State Election Commission (https://www.dvk-rs.si/volitve-in-referendumi/drzavni-zbor-rs/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors 65–70% voter turnout for Slovenia's 2026 parliamentary election, mirroring the 69.7% seen in the competitive 2022 vote that ousted Janez Janša's government. This range leads due to historical patterns—turnout dipped below 55% in 2014–2018 amid fragmentation but rebounded with heightened polarization under Robert Golob's Freedom Movement administration. Lower bins trail as recent public opinion surveys indicate stable civic engagement, without signs of widespread apathy. Recent developments, including EU-aligned policy pushes on migration and green energy, sustain moderate enthusiasm, though no major catalysts like scandals have shifted dynamics yet; traders eye 2025 local elections for early signals.

If the results of this election are not known by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

This market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Slovenian Government, specifically the State Election Commission (https://www.dvk-rs.si/volitve-in-referendumi/drzavni-zbor-rs/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors 65–70% voter turnout for Slovenia's 2026 parliamentary election, mirroring the 69.7% seen in the competitive 2022 vote that ousted Janez Janša's government. This range leads due to historical patterns—turnout dipped below 55% in 2014–2018 amid fragmentation but rebounded with heightened polarization under Robert Golob's Freedom Movement administration. Lower bins trail as recent public opinion surveys indicate stable civic engagement, without signs of widespread apathy. Recent developments, including EU-aligned policy pushes on migration and green energy, sustain moderate enthusiasm, though no major catalysts like scandals have shifted dynamics yet; traders eye 2025 local elections for early signals.

question: US military draft authorized in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any legislation is passed by both houses of the United States Congress and signed into law by the president, or otherwise enacted, which authorizes the induction of personnel into the United States Armed Forces through activation of the United States Selective Service System, or otherwise establishes a military draft, by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Legislation that only modifies Selective Service registration requirements or administrative procedures without authorizing induction/conscription into the armed forces will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the United States federal government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Absence of any congressional bills, executive actions, or official Defense Department proposals to reinstate the military draft underpins the 91.5% implied probability for "No" on authorization in 2026. The U.S. has maintained an all-volunteer force since 1973, with Selective Service registration continuing as a dormant contingency but no activation mechanism advanced. Recent Army recruitment shortfalls have prompted incentives like higher pay and bonuses rather than draft discussions, as confirmed in Pentagon budget requests and bipartisan congressional hearings. No major political figures or 2025 National Defense Authorization Act drafts signal change, reinforcing trader consensus amid stable geopolitical priorities.

question: Sabres vs. Rangers
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 8 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Sabres win, the market will resolve to "Sabres".
If the Rangers win, the market will resolve to "Rangers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rangers' superior Metropolitan Division standing and 7-2-1 home record drive trader consensus heavily favoring New York over Buffalo, implying strong win probabilities amid the Sabres' 4-7-3 slump and middling 2-4-2 road play. Igor Shesterkin's elite .928 save percentage in November anchors the Blueshirts' defense, fresh off a 4-1-0 streak, while Ukko-Pekka Luukkonen's heavy workload (15 starts) shows cracks with a .895 save rate lately. Tage Thompson's return boosts Buffalo's top line, but their penalty kill ranks 25th league-wide, vulnerable against New York's power play. This rivalry matchup at Madison Square Garden carries playoff implications, with rest edge to Rangers post-back-to-back avoidance.

Home-ice advantage and Leafs' 6-2 head-to-head record since 2022 further tilt implied probabilities, though Caps' Ovechkin chases Gretzky's goal mark on the road. eventMetadata.context_description: Edmonton Oilers enter their matchup against the San Jose Sharks as heavy favorites, with trader consensus implying around 75% win probability, driven by their explosive 5-2-0 start fueled by Connor McDavid's six goals in seven games and a three-game win streak. The Sharks, mired at 2-3-2 with defensive lapses allowing 4.0 goals per game, host at SAP Center but face a rested Oilers squad after a day off. Key injuries include Sharks' top defender Erik Karlsson questionable with a upper-body issue, while Oilers' depth holds firm despite long-term absences like Adam Larsson. Historical dominance—Oilers 7-1-2 in last 10 meetings—bolsters sentiment, though Sharks' home desperation could spark an upset.

events.title: Oilers vs. Sharks
events.description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 8 at 10:00PM ET:
If the Oilers win, the market will resolve to "Oilers".
If the Sharks win, the market will resolve to "Sharks".

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Edmonton Oilers enter their matchup against the San Jose Sharks as heavy favorites, with trader consensus implying around 75% win probability, driven by their explosive 5-2-0 start fueled by Connor McDavid's six goals in seven games and a three-game win streak. The Sharks, mired at 2-3-2 with defensive lapses allowing 4.0 goals per game, host at SAP Center but face a rested Oilers squad after a day off. Key injuries include Sharks' top defender Erik Karlsson questionable with a upper-body issue, while Oilers' depth holds firm despite long-term absences like Adam Larsson. Historical dominance—Oilers 7-1-2 in last 10 meetings—bolsters sentiment, though Sharks' home desperation could spark an upset.

question: Will Social Democrats win the second most seats in the Danish Folketing in the 2026 Danish general election?
description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the second-greatest number of seats in the Danish Folketing (unicameral national legislature) as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party name. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Danish Folketing in this election.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Danish government sources such as the Ministry of Interior and Health (https://www.valg.im.dk/) and Statistics Denmark (https://www.dst.dk/en).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls consistently place the Green Left as the runner-up to the frontrunning Social Democrats in vote share for Denmark's next parliamentary election, fueling trader consensus at 76% implied probability for their 2nd-place finish. Aggregated surveys from major pollsters show Green Left polling around 12-14%, well ahead of Liberal Alliance (4-5%), Venstre (8-9%), and Moderates (9-10%), with margins solidifying their position amid stable left-of-center support. Recent developments, including the Green Left's gains in post-debate surveys and voter shifts from smaller leftist parties, have widened their lead, while Liberal Alliance draws bets on potential youth turnout surges and Venstre faces headwinds from rural discontent. Uncertainty persists given the 2026 election timeline and possible early catalysts like economic data or coalition shifts.

question: Will turnout in the 2026 Danish general elections be below 80%?
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve based on the official voter turnout rate for the 2026 Danish General Election, defined as the total number of votes cast divided by the total number of eligible voters. The turnout rate shall be based solely on the results from Denmark proper, excluding the results and eligible voter counts from Greenland and the Faroe Islands.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

If the results of this election are not known by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

This market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Danish Government, specifically Statistics Denmark (https://www.dst.dk/) or Valg (https://www.valg.dk/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Historical voter turnout in Denmark's parliamentary elections, averaging 84-86% over the past decade—including 84.1% in 2022 and 84.6% in 2019—anchors trader consensus toward the 80–85% (56.5%) and 85–90% (38.5%) outcomes, reflecting the nation's strong civic participation without compulsory voting. Recent political stability under Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats, coupled with steady early polling, supports this range, as no major catalysts like scandals or reforms have emerged to suggest deviation. Discussions on youth engagement and online voting trials remain nascent, with the election due by mid-2026; traders price in base rates amid low uncertainty.

Recent developments, including the Green Left's gains in post-debate surveys and voter shifts from smaller leftist parties, have widened their lead, while Liberal Alliance draws bets on potential youth turnout surges and Venstre faces headwinds from rural discontent. Uncertainty persists given the 2026 election timeline and possible early catalysts like economic data or coalition shifts.

question: Will Social Democrats win the third most seats in the Danish Folketing in the 2026 Danish general election?
description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Danish Folketing (unicameral national legislature) as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party name. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Danish Folketing in this election.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Danish government sources such as the Ministry of Interior and Health (https://www.valg.im.dk/) and Statistics Denmark (https://www.dst.dk/en).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Denmark parliamentary election prices the Liberal Alliance at 45% implied probability for third place by vote share, reflecting recent polls positioning it strongly behind projected leaders Social Democrats and Venstre. Nationwide surveys like the early October Voxmeter poll show Social Democrats at 25.4%, Venstre at 15.9%, and Liberal Alliance at 11.1%, with Denmark Democrats (8.8%), Moderates (9.4%), and Green Left (8.1%) clustered closely behind. The Danish People’s Party holds 6.8% but trails amid declining nationalist support. This tight mid-pack race, unchanged materially in the past week, underscores trader bets on Liberal Alliance's steady polling amid center-right fragmentation, though the election—due by late 2026—remains vulnerable to economic shifts or snap-call risks.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Danish Folketing (unicameral national legislature) as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party name. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Danish Folketing in this election.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Danish government sources such as the Ministry of Interior and Health (https://www.valg.im.dk/) and Statistics Denmark (https://www.dst.dk/en).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Denmark parliamentary election prices the Liberal Alliance at 45% implied probability for third place by vote share, reflecting recent polls positioning it strongly behind projected leaders Social Democrats and Venstre. Nationwide surveys like the early October Voxmeter poll show Social Democrats at 25.4%, Venstre at 15.9%, and Liberal Alliance at 11.1%, with Denmark Democrats (8.8%), Moderates (9.4%), and Green Left (8.1%) clustered closely behind. The Danish People’s Party holds 6.8% but trails amid declining nationalist support. This tight mid-pack race, unchanged materially in the past week, underscores trader bets on Liberal Alliance's steady polling amid center-right fragmentation, though the election—due by late 2026—remains vulnerable to economic shifts or snap-call risks.

question: US x Iran ceasefire by April 15?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Escalating Israel-Iran exchanges and Iran's proxy attacks via Hezbollah and Houthis drive low trader consensus for a US-Iran ceasefire, as Washington prioritizes Israel's security amid stalled nuclear talks. Recent developments include Israel's October 26 strikes on Iranian missile sites following Tehran's limited barrage, with US forces aiding defenses but urging restraint to avoid wider war. Biden administration channels through Oman yielded no breakthroughs, per official readouts, while Tehran demands sanctions relief unmet by demands for verifiable nuclear curbs. Upcoming US election results could pivot policy—Trump favors pressure, Harris continuity—keeping odds depressed amid high uncertainty in diplomatic momentum.

question: Will the total number of TSA passengers for March 16 - March 22 be less than 17,500,000?
description:
This market will resolve to the total number of TSA passengers reported between March 16, 2026 and March 22, 2026 (inclusive). 

The total number of TSA passengers will be calculated by summing all of the TSA daily checkpoint throughputs reported for this date range (e.g. if there were a daily TSA checkpoint throughput of 2 million reported on December 1 and a daily TSA checkpoint throughput of 3 million reported on December 2, the total number of TSA passengers for December 1-2 would be 5 million).

If the reported total number of TSA passengers falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve as soon as throughput data becomes available for all dates within the listed range. Any revisions published to data for dates December 8, 2025 and onward prior to the release of data for all dates within the listed range will be considered.

If data is not available for any of the dates from March 16, 2026 to March 22, 2026 by April 30, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the daily checkpoint throughputs as measured by the US Transportation Security Administration (TSA), https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes. Should this URL change or move locations, a new URL on the tsa.gov domain will remain valid to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices TSA passenger volumes for March 16-22 in a tight race between 18-18.5 million (49%) and 18.5-19 million (44.5%), driven by the prior week's 18.2 million total—up 5% from the week before amid rising spring break demand and robust domestic air travel recovery. Steady economic indicators, full airline load factors, and mild weather supported higher throughput, but uncertainty lingers over the exact timing of vacation surges and any last-minute capacity constraints. The narrow spread reflects sequential trends from February's 17-18 million averages, with lower buckets fading as no disruptions emerged. Separation could come from preliminary airline booking data or weekend screening tallies hinting at acceleration.

The total number of TSA passengers will be calculated by summing all of the TSA daily checkpoint throughputs reported for this date range (e.g. if there were a daily TSA checkpoint throughput of 2 million reported on December 1 and a daily TSA checkpoint throughput of 3 million reported on December 2, the total number of TSA passengers for December 1-2 would be 5 million).

If the reported total number of TSA passengers falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve as soon as throughput data becomes available for all dates within the listed range. Any revisions published to data for dates December 8, 2025 and onward prior to the release of data for all dates within the listed range will be considered.

If data is not available for any of the dates from March 16, 2026 to March 22, 2026 by April 30, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the daily checkpoint throughputs as measured by the US Transportation Security Administration (TSA), https://www.tsa.gov/travel/passenger-volumes. Should this URL change or move locations, a new URL on the tsa.gov domain will remain valid to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices TSA passenger volumes for March 16-22 in a tight race between 18-18.5 million (49%) and 18.5-19 million (44.5%), driven by the prior week's 18.2 million total—up 5% from the week before amid rising spring break demand and robust domestic air travel recovery. Steady economic indicators, full airline load factors, and mild weather supported higher throughput, but uncertainty lingers over the exact timing of vacation surges and any last-minute capacity constraints. The narrow spread reflects sequential trends from February's 17-18 million averages, with lower buckets fading as no disruptions emerged. Separation could come from preliminary airline booking data or weekend screening tallies hinting at acceleration.

question: US x Iran meeting by April 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket shows low implied probability for a US-Iran diplomatic meeting in the near term, driven by persistent Middle East tensions from Houthi Red Sea attacks, Israel-Hezbollah clashes, and Iran's nuclear advancements nearing weapons-grade uranium levels. Indirect Oman-mediated talks in August focused on sanctions and nuclear curbs but produced no direct bilateral commitment, despite reformist President Pezeshkian's calls for engagement. US officials insist on verifiable compliance before easing pressure, maintaining a pattern unbroken since pre-2018 negotiations. Key upcoming catalysts include IAEA monitoring reports in November and the US presidential election, which could recalibrate diplomatic postures amid high uncertainty.

question: Will Tisza win <70 seats in the Hungarian National Assembly in this election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the number of seats won by TISZA in the Hungarian National Assembly (Országgyűlés) as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the specified party, not any coalition of which it may be a part.

For parties that contest some seats jointly with another party, including as part of a joint national party list, the number of seats won by the specified party will be determined based on a consensus of credible reporting. If necessary, official information identifying the party affiliation of specific elected candidates, including candidate registration records, parliamentary party member lists, and similar official sources, will also be used.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based on the official results as reported by the Hungarian government, specifically the Hungarian Election Authority (Nemzeti Választási Iroda) (valasztas.hu).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls showing Péter Magyar's TISZA party neck-and-neck with Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's Fidesz—often leading 35-45% to 28-35% in list votes—anchor trader consensus around 90-119 seats for TISZA in Hungary's 199-seat parliament, reflecting uncertainty in translating national support into single-member district wins where Fidesz holds rural advantages and gerrymandering edges. TISZA's anti-corruption momentum from Magyar's scandals exposé and mass rallies has narrowed the gap since spring 2024, but fragmented opposition and low turnout risks keep projections tight. Separation could emerge from upcoming candidate lists, EU fund disputes escalating voter turnout, or Fidesz policy shifts on inflation and Ukraine aid ahead of the April 2026 vote.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. question: Will voter turnout be <65% in the 2026 Hungarian parliamentary election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve based on the official voter turnout rate for the 2026 Hungary Parliamentary election, defined as the total number of votes cast divided by the total number of eligible voters. All eligible voters includes eligible voters voting by mail or without a Hungarian address.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

If the results of this election are not known by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

This market will resolve based on the official results as reported by the Hungarian government, including the Hungarian Election Authority (Nemzeti Választási Iroda) (valasztas.hu).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the 2026 Hungary parliamentary election turnout clusters around 68–77%, mirroring historical levels of 70% in 2018 and 73% in 2022, as Fidesz faces a resurgent opposition led by Péter Magyar's Tisza party, which surged in recent polls and the 2024 EU vote with 60% turnout. This tight race persists due to balanced expectations on voter mobilization—high participation historically benefits incumbents via rural strongholds, but urban discontent over inflation and governance could boost anti-Fidesz turnout without clear polling separation yet. Catalysts for shifts include fresh nationwide surveys, economic data releases, or alliance announcements, with traders pricing modest tails for extremes above 80% or below 65% based on mobilization intensity.

question: Will Russia enter Novyi Donbas by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Novyi Donbas, Donetsk Oblast, (48.450718° N, 37.181238° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have made incremental gains in Donetsk Oblast, capturing villages near Novyi Donbas amid intensified assaults on the Pokrovsk axis, but have not yet entered the settlement according to frontline reports from the Institute for the Study of War. Ukrainian defenses, bolstered by recent Western aid including artillery shells, continue to inflict heavy casualties on advancing Russian units, slowing momentum despite Moscow's numerical superiority. Trader consensus reflects uncertainty over Russia's ability to breach fortified positions before the market deadline, with odds incorporating base rates from prior slow advances like those around Avdiivka. Upcoming events include potential U.S. aid packages and winter weather impacts, which could further constrain operations.

question: GPT-5.5 released by April 15, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI's GPT-5.5 model is made available to the general public by the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

GPT-5.5 refers to a product explicitly named GPT-5.5, or a variant that is recognized as a direct successor to GPT-5.4, similar to the progression from GPT-5.1 to GPT-5.2. (e.g., GPT-5.6, GPT-5.7, etc would qualify toward a "Yes" resolution to this market)

Qualifying releases of task-specialized models (e.g., GPT-Codex/Transcribe), cost-efficiency variants (e.g., Nano/Mini), or reasoning models of the o-series family will count for this market. Products labeled as a new flagship generation GPT-6 or similar will NOT qualify.

For this market to resolve to "Yes," a qualifying model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. 

The release must be either clearly defined and publicly announced by OpenAI as being accessible to the general public or otherwise made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled within the company’s official website. Labeling errors, placeholder text, or version names displayed on the website that do not correspond to a model that is actually accessible to the general public will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from OpenAI, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Spread: Armenia (-2.5)
description:
In the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 26 at 11:25 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Armenia" if Armenia win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "United Arab Emirates".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. question: Will there be exactly 0 earthquakes of magnitude 6.5 or higher worldwide by March 22?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of earthquakes with a magnitude of 6.5 or higher that occur anywhere on Earth between March 16, 2026, 12:00 AM ET, and March 22, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Earthquake Hazards Program (https://earthquake.usgs.gov/earthquakes/browse/significant.php#sigdef).

If an earthquake of substantial size has occurred within this market's timeframe but not yet appeared on the resolution source, this market may remain open until April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or until the earthquake in question otherwise appears on the resolution source. If such an earthquake has not appeared on the resolution source by that date, another credible resolution source will be used.

This market may not resolve until the timeframe of this market has concluded. If a qualifying earthquake has been recorded on the final day, this market may remain open for 24 hours to allow for revisions to the recorded magnitude. After 24 hours, this market will resolve according to the latest provided data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors exactly two magnitude 6.5 or greater earthquakes from March 16-22, driven by USGS-confirmed events—a 6.9 off Vanuatu on March 17 and a 6.6 near the Loyalty Islands on March 20—aligning with the global historical average of about 2-3 such quakes per week based on long-term seismic catalogs. With only days remaining and no active aftershock clusters or heightened foreshock activity in high-risk zones like the Pacific Ring of Fire, the Poisson-distributed nature of seismicity implies low probability for a third. A realistic challenge would require a sudden swarm in a prolific area like Indonesia or the Marianas, though current monitoring shows subdued strain accumulation.

question: DeepSeek V4 released by April 15?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next DeepSeek V model is made available to the general public by April 15, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Intermediate versions (e.g., DeepSeek-V3.5) will not count; however, versions such as DeepSeek V4 or V5 would count.

The "next DeepSeek V model" refers to the next major release in the DeepSeek V series, explicitly named as such or clearly positioned as a successor to DeepSeek-V3.

Only releases representing a core version progression in the DeepSeek V series, “clearly positioned as a successor to DeepSeek-V3,” will qualify. Other models, such as derivative models (e.g., "V4-Lite," "V4-Mini"), task-specialized models, R-series reasoning models, and experimental or preview releases (e.g., "V4-Exp," "V4-Preview"), that are not positioned as the new V flagship model, will not qualify.

For this market to resolve to "Yes," the next DeepSeek V model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be clearly defined and publicly announced by DeepSeek as being accessible to the general public.

If a qualifying model is made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled with the relevant version name within the company’s official website, this will qualify as “publicly announced”. Labeling errors, placeholder text, or version names displayed on the website that do not correspond to a model that is actually accessible to the general public under the rules will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from DeepSeek, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

Intermediate versions (e.g., DeepSeek-V3.5) will not count; however, versions such as DeepSeek V4 or V5 would count.

For this market to resolve to "Yes," the next DeepSeek V model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be clearly defined and publicly announced by DeepSeek as being accessible to the general public.

The "next DeepSeek V model" refers to the next major release in the DeepSeek V series, explicitly named as such or clearly positioned as a successor to DeepSeek-V3.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from DeepSeek, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on DeepSeek V4 release odds reflects caution amid the Chinese AI lab's rapid iteration cycle, with implied probabilities hovering below 20% for a Q1 2025 launch following DeepSeek-V3's December 2024 debut as a top open-weight model rivaling GPT-4o. Primary drivers include High-Flyer Capital-backed DeepSeek's January confirmation of V4 training on massive 10T+ token datasets, but no firm timeline amid hardware constraints and U.S. chip export curbs impacting Nvidia H100 access. Competitive pressure from Meta's Llama 4 and Mistral's next-gen models tempers optimism, while upcoming Lunar New Year delays and potential regulatory scrutiny from MIIT could push timelines; watch for API previews or GitHub drops as key resolution catalysts.

question: Will Trump say "Egghead" this week? (March 22)
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between March 16, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and March 22, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

question: Czechia vs. Republic of Ireland: O/U 1.5
description:
In the upcoming World Cup Europe Qualifiers game between Czechia and Republic of Ireland, scheduled for March 26 at 3:45 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Czechia and Republic of Ireland combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.description: More markets for the World Cup Europe Qualifiers game, scheduled for March 26 at 3:45 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Czechia in their UEFA Nations League clash with Republic of Ireland, driven by the Czechs' strong home form and Ireland's defensive vulnerabilities, implying around 55-60% win probability across related markets. Momentum shifts could tighten if Ireland recalls Evan Ferguson up top.

events.title: Italy vs. Northern Ireland - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the World Cup Europe Qualifiers game, scheduled for March 26 at 3:45 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Italy enters as heavy trader favorite against Northern Ireland, with market odds reflecting their superior talent depth and home-soil edge at Stadio Friuli. Recent Nations League form bolsters sentiment: Italy's 3-1 win over France showcased midfield dominance from Barella and Frattesi, while Northern Ireland slumped 1-3 to Romania amid defensive lapses. Key absences hurt the visitors—no McNair in defense—while Italy's full squad rotation post-internationals provides freshness. Historical head-to-head favors Azzurri (4-0 aggregate last decade), though Northern Ireland's counterattack grit could test over/under goals. Watch lineup confirmations; rest advantages tilt probabilities firmly Italy's way amid playoff-irrelevant stakes.

events.title: Wales vs. Bosnia and Herzegovina - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the World Cup Europe Qualifiers game, scheduled for March 26 at 3:45 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Wales commands trader consensus as favorites in their UEFA Nations League Group B3 home clash against Bosnia and Herzegovina at Cardiff City Stadium, fueled by a perfect start with two wins and six points atop the standings. Bosnia sits third with just one point, hampered by a 1-2 loss to Northern Ireland and a winless run in five away qualifiers. Wales leverages unbeaten home form across five recent internationals, with Callum Wilson and Harry Wilson in sharp scoring touch despite defensive injury concerns like Ben Davies' absence. Bosnia's aging squad lacks Dzeko's firepower, exposing vulnerabilities against set pieces. Stakes rise with Iceland looming next, amplifying Wales' momentum edge in this pivotal matchup.
